caveats: Public showcase only. No real patient data, secure AHS data, private Snowflake data, or private AHS endpoints are used.
Internal ED operating state, model outputs, scenario effects, and unit-level operating values are synthetic demonstration values.
Public facts are used only where cited. Unit-level values without public evidence are labelled synthetic planning assumptions.

data.name: Typical winter respiratory surge
data.description: Moderate RSV/influenza rise with school-week pressure.

data.name: Severe RSV week
data.description: High RSV, respiratory cohorting, and PICU-sensitive demand.

data.name: Smoke plus respiratory sensitivity
data.description: AQHI deterioration layered onto respiratory-vulnerable demand.

data.name: Snowstorm access disruption
data.description: Access friction and lumpy arrivals with offload sensitivity.

data.name: Boarding-heavy day
data.description: Inpatient receiving pressure dominates the ED operating picture.

data.name: Staffing shortfall
data.description: ED nursing, physician, and triage capacity under baseline.

data.name: Discharge acceleration
data.description: Bed release, cleaning, and early huddle pull capacity forward.

data.name: Fast-track expansion
data.description: Low-acuity streaming and extended fast-track hours.

data.name: Rapid assessment intervention
data.description: Front-door physician/rapid-assessment capacity to reduce initial waits.

data.name: Combined high-pressure week
data.description: Respiratory, access, staffing, and boarding pressure at once.
